verses.text: God bids the prophet 'cry aloud' and show the people their sin; they fast and seek Him daily, yet complain He takes no notice. Their religion is outward. Mere ritual, without a right heart, earns no answer from God.

God bids the prophet 'cry aloud' and show the people their sin; they fast and seek Him daily, yet complain He takes no notice. Their religion is outward. Mere ritual, without a right heart, earns no answer from God.

God bids the prophet 'cry aloud' and show the people their sin; they fast and seek Him daily, yet complain He takes no notice. Their religion is outward. Mere ritual, without a right heart, earns no answer from God.

Their fasts end in strife and pretence; is true fasting only 'to bow down his head as a bulrush'? Self-display is not devotion. God is not honoured by a show of humility that does not touch the heart.

Their fasts end in strife and pretence; is true fasting only 'to bow down his head as a bulrush'? Self-display is not devotion. God is not honoured by a show of humility that does not touch the heart.

'Is not this the fast that I have chosen? to loose the bands of wickedness... to deal thy bread to the hungry,' and clothe the naked. True fasting acts in mercy. God values justice and kindness above empty abstinence.

'Is not this the fast that I have chosen? to loose the bands of wickedness... to deal thy bread to the hungry,' and clothe the naked. True fasting acts in mercy. God values justice and kindness above empty abstinence.

Then 'shall thy light break forth as the morning,' thy healing spring up, and 'thou shalt cry, and he shall say, Here I am.' Mercy opens heaven. The God who seemed silent answers the one who shows compassion.

Then 'shall thy light break forth as the morning,' thy healing spring up, and 'thou shalt cry, and he shall say, Here I am.' Mercy opens heaven. The God who seemed silent answers the one who shows compassion.

If you pour yourself out for the hungry, your light shall rise in darkness, and you shall be called 'the repairer of the breach.' Self-giving brings blessing. Those who serve the needy become builders and healers of their land.

If you pour yourself out for the hungry, your light shall rise in darkness, and you shall be called 'the repairer of the breach.' Self-giving brings blessing. Those who serve the needy become builders and healers of their land.

If you pour yourself out for the hungry, your light shall rise in darkness, and you shall be called 'the repairer of the breach.' Self-giving brings blessing. Those who serve the needy become builders and healers of their land.

If you honour the sabbath, not seeking your own pleasure, 'then shalt thou delight thyself in the LORD,' and ride on the high places. Honouring God's day brings joy. Delight in God is the reward of those who keep His holy rest.

If you honour the sabbath, not seeking your own pleasure, 'then shalt thou delight thyself in the LORD,' and ride on the high places. Honouring God's day brings joy. Delight in God is the reward of those who keep His holy rest.
